title: Dynamics connects net force to acceleration
statement: For 9702 dynamics problems, the central relation is that the resultant force causes acceleration in the same direction, with larger net force producing larger acceleration for the same mass.

title: Wave speed links frequency and wavelength
statement: A wave question can usually be anchored by the relation wave speed equals frequency multiplied by wavelength, with unit consistency checked before any substitution.

title: Potential difference is energy transferred per unit charge
statement: In 9702 electricity, potential difference is interpreted as energy transferred per unit charge, which helps connect circuit reasoning to power and resistance calculations.

title: Dynamics method: resolve forces before applying Newton's second law
statement: A stable transformed dynamics method is to identify the object, draw the force directions, resolve components if needed, and only then apply the force-resultant equation along the chosen axis.

title: Electricity method: chain current, resistance, and power carefully
statement: A stable transformed electricity method is to decide which pair of circuit quantities is known first, derive the missing current or potential difference, and then use the consistent form of Ohm's law or power before rounding at the end.
